title: phymatosorus grossus
intro: Phymatosorus grossus, also known as the Indian tuckahoe or tuckahoe fern, is a type of pteridophyte, a plant that produces spores instead of seeds, native to the Himalayas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Phymatosorus grossus include its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions, among others. Phymatosorus grossus is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, and it can also be used as a poultice or a topical treatment for skin conditions.
